steps.text: Compute the means. Data set A: nine 5s. Mean$_A = \frac{9\times5}{9} = 5$. Data set B: nine 5s plus one 100. Mean$_B = \frac{9\times5+100}{10} = \frac{145}{10} = 14.5$. The means are different.

Compute the medians. Data set A has 9 values; the median is the 5th value when sorted: all are 5, so Median$_A = 5$. Data set B has 10 values sorted as 5,5,5,5,5,5,5,5,5,100; the median is the average of the 5th and 6th values: $\frac{5+5}{2}=5$. So Median$_B = 5$. The medians are the same.

Evaluate each choice:
  • Choice A — Only the means are different.: the means do differ (5 vs 14.5) and the medians are both 5. That confirms A is correct.
  • Choice B — Only the medians are different.: the medians are both 5 (not different), and the means are different. Incorrect.
  • Choice C — Both the means and the medians are different.: the medians are equal, so this is false. Incorrect.
  • Choice D — Neither the means nor the medians are different.: the means do differ, so this is false. Incorrect.
